mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers the peoples and civilizations of mainland and island Southeast Asia, the core subject of this entire section.
Relevant to subsections B1c, B2b, B3c-e, and C2 on the central role of Theravada Buddhism in Burma, Siam, Cambodia, and Srivijaya.
Relevant to subsections B3a, C1, and C3-5 on Indian Hindu cultural influences on Funan, Cambodia, and the Indonesian kingdoms.
Relevant to subsections B5a and C on the advent of Islam in Malaya and the Indonesian Archipelago.
Relevant to subsection B1c on the Mongol destruction of Pagan in 1287 and Mongol interactions with Southeast Asian states.
Relevant to subsection B5b on early European intrusions including the Portuguese conquest of Malacca in 1511.
Relevant to subsection C2 on the maritime trade between Srivijaya and China and broader trade networks connecting Southeast Asia.
Relevant to the Portuguese and Spanish arrival in Southeast Asia discussed in subsections B3b-c and B5b.
Relevant to subsection A on the archaeological sources for understanding prehistoric Southeast Asian civilizations.
